LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK'S MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING RECIEVER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 302 PER JUDGE BUSCH'S RECUSAL. MATTER WILL BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 302 ON AUGUST 5, 2009. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189
ORDER RE: PLAINTIFF FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK'S EVID. OBJ. TO DEFENDANT DINESH MANIAR'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR AN ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER AND PRELIMARY INJUNCTION
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-05-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK, MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEFAULTED UNDER THE LOANS, THE LOAN DOCUMENTS AUTHORIZE THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER. CONSTRUCTION OF IMPROVEMENTS ON THE PROPERTY HAVE CEASED, SEVERAL MECHANIC LIENS ARE RECORDED ON THE PROPERTY, AND AT LEAST THREE COMPLAINTS TO FORECLOSE MECHANICS LIENS HAVE BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT OWES PLAINTIFF APPROXIMATELY $6,880,000.00 AND THE PROPERTY IN ITS CURRENT CONDITION IS PROBABLY INSUFFICIENT TO DISCHARGE THE DEBT. PLAINTIFF WILL SUFFER WASTE AND IRREPARABLE HARM WITHOUT THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. DAMAGES NOT ASCERTAINABLE AND WOULD NOT AFFORD ADEQUATE RELIEF. RECEIVER TO FILE A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $75,000.00. PLAINTIFF TO FILE A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $75,000.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Reciever And Preliminary Injunction; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Aug-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK, Motion for Order Appointing Receiver and Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Jess R. Bressi, Esq. Luce Forward, (949)2418967, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; James. T. Graeb, Esq, & Edward Stepans, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 595-4348, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. Defendant defaulted under the loans, the loan documents authorize the appointment of a receiver. Construction of improvements on the property have ceased, several mechanic liens are recorded on the property, and at least three complaints to foreclose mechanics liens have been filed. Defendant owes plaintiff approximately $6,880,000.00 and the property in its current condition is probably insufficient to discharge the debt. Plaintiff will suffer waste and irreparable harm without the appointment of a receiver and preliminary injunction. Damages not ascertainable and would not afford adequate relief. Receiver to file a bond in the amount of $75,000.00. Plaintiff to file a bond in the amount of $75,000.00. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
